title: Morgan Stanley files bond offering in latest bank to raise capital
text: Morgan Stanley MS, -2.34% is offering two tranches of bonds as the latest in a series of debt deals from big banks, according to a filing. Morgan Stanley plans to sell global medium-term notes, Series I.

title: Truist raising $1.75B in bond offering, making it the latest bank to raise capital
text: Truist Financial Corp. is pricing a $1.75 billion bond offering with strong demand on Wednesday, a source in the bond market told MarketWatch.

title: Truist to speak at the BancAnalysts Association of Boston Conference
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Oct. 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) today announced that Chief Financial Officer Mike Maguire will speak at the BancAnalysts Association of Boston Conference on Friday, Nov. 3, 2023, at 9:50 a.m.

title: Truist declares common and preferred stock dividends
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , Oct. 24, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Board of Directors of Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.52 per common share, payable on Dec. 1, 2023, to shareholders of record at the close of business on Nov. 10, 2023.

title: Truist Bank Partners Trovata to Enhance Liquidity Management
text: Truist Bank has partnered with Trovata, a provider of bank application programming interfaces (APIs) and enterprise cash management services, to enhance cash forecasting and liquidity management for its commercial and corporate clients.

title: Truist Partners with Trovata to Provide Clients Automated Cash Forecasting & Liquidity Management Solutions
text: SAN DIEGO , Oct. 24, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Trovata, the global leader in bank APIs and enterprise cash management, today announced a partnership agreement with Truist Bank (NYSE: TFC). Now, Truist commercial and corporate clients will benefit from improved access to Trovata's cash management platform to modernize cash positioning and forecasting workflows through data-driven automation.

title: Regional Lenders Are Getting Smaller to Stay Afloat
text: America's regional banks are reportedly hoping to get healthier by getting smaller. As The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported Monday (Oct. 23), these lenders are scaling back following a quarter that saw banks like KeyCorp, Truist and Citizens Financial record double-digit declines in profit.

title: Regional Bank Stocks' Gains Belie Their Q3 Results
text: From a stock market standpoint, regional U.S. banks have stabilized after this spring's banking turmoil. But that recovery belies their deteriorating financial performance.

title: Truist: 60% of Transactions Are Now Digital
text: Truist says it is seeing a jump in digital engagement as it continues its cost-cutting measures. Speaking during an earnings call Thursday (Oct. 19) CEO Bill Rogers said the regional lender's digital transactions jumped 9% year over year, fueled largely by Zelle users, with those transactions up 32% since last year.

title: Truist Bank: Top Value Pick From Beaten-Down Banks
text: Truist Financial has taken prudent action to maintain and improve its capital positioning and secure its dividend amidst the bank turmoil of early 2023. The reaction by private companies and federal funding to the bank crisis suggests that a bank of Truist's size would not be allowed to fail.

title: Truist (TFC) Mulls Selling Remaining Insurance Stake, Stock Up
text: Truist (TFC) mulls divesting the remaining 80% stake in the insurance subsidiary, Truist Insurance Holdings, to strengthen the balance sheet. The news cheers investors, and shares rise 6.6%.

title: Truist Financial stock up on report of $10B unit sale to Stone Point
text: Truist Financial Corp. TFC, +0.40% was up 2.5% in premarket trading on Tuesday after a report by Semafor that it's in negotiations to sell the remaining 80% of its insurance brokerage business for $10 billion to Stone Point. The private-equity firm already acquired 20% of the business earlier this year and is in talks to buy the remaining stake, according to the report, which cited unnamed people familiar with the deal.
